LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT C3 PATIENTS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT - THE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IS DENIED. FIRST, DEFENDANT APPEARS TO BE AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION AND, AS SUCH, MUST BE REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL IN ORDER TO APPEAR IN COURT. THE ENTITY CANNOT REPRESENT ITSELF IN PRO PER. SEE CLEAN AIR TRANSPORT SYSTEM V. SAN MATEO (1988) 198 CAL.APP.3D 576, 578. IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES PROPER SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ON DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)
PLAINTIFF(S) REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR BRUCE MARSHAL LUBARSKY.

(206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Department 206 for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up. Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Ronald Losch, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff AFI Investment, Inc.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendant C3 Patients Association.
Witness Theresa Zabit appearing for Plaintiff sworn and examined.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-10 dismissed without prejudice.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff AFI Investment, Inc and against Defendant C3 Patients Association as follows:
Principal $25,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest $5,416.50
Attorney's Fees $10,000.00
Costs $486.90
Total Judgment in the amount of $40,903.40

Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT C3 PATIENTS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint -
The motion to quash service of the summons and complaint is denied. First, defendant appears to be an unincorporated association and, as such, must be represented by counsel in order to appear in court. The entity cannot represent itself in pro per. See Clean Air Transport System v. San Mateo (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 576, 578. In any event, plaintiff establishes proper service of the summons and complaint on defendant. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =302/MJM

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Continued To Aug-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT C3 PATIENTS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is continued to August 7, 2014 per stipulation of parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
